info.title: On-Call Health API
info.description: API for monitoring team wellbeing and detecting burnout risk in engineering teams

get.description: Root endpoint.
Health check endpoint.
Initiate Google OAuth login.
Handle Google OAuth callback.
Initiate GitHub OAuth login.
Handle GitHub OAuth callback.
Get current user information.
Get all OAuth providers linked to current user.
Get all emails for current user.

delete.description: Unlink an OAuth provider from current user.

get.description:
✅ SECURITY: Get current authenticated user basic information.
Used to verify authentication works.

post.description:
✅ ENTERPRISE PATTERN: Exchange temporary auth code for JWT token.

This implements the industry-standard 2-step OAuth token exchange:
1. OAuth callback creates temporary auth code
2. Frontend securely exchanges code for JWT token

patch.description:
Update a user's role within the organization.
Only admin can change roles.

schema.title: Response Update User Role Auth Users  User Id  Role Patch

get.description:
Get list of team members who can be promoted to admin.
Only returns users with active accounts (not just UserCorrelations).
Used when sole admin needs to promote someone before deleting their account.

schema.title: Response Get Promotable Users Auth Users Promotable Get

delete.description:
Delete the current user's account and all associated data.

This is a permanent, irreversible action that will:
- Delete all analyses
- Delete all burnout reports (self-assessments)
- Delete all notifications
- Delete all survey preferences
- Delete all integrations (Rootly, PagerDuty, GitHub, Slack, Jira)
- Delete OAuth providers
- Delete email addresses
- Delete user correlations and mappings
- Nullify organization invitations sent by this user
- Delete the user account itself

Requires email confirmation for safety.

get.description: Get all members and pending invitations for the current user's organization.

schema.title: Response Get Organization Members Auth Organizations Members Get

delete.description:
Remove a member from the organization. Admin only.

Safety checks:
- Only admins can remove members
- Cannot remove yourself
- Cannot remove if you're the last admin
- Sets user's organization_id to NULL (user becomes org-less)
- Deletes user's API keys for security

schema.title: Response Remove Organization Member Auth Organizations Members  User Id  Delete

post.description:
Password-based login for E2E testing.
NOT intended for production use - OAuth is the primary authentication method.

Security: Uses constant-time operations to prevent timing attacks.

get.description: Test the current user's Rootly API token.

post.description: Test Rootly token and return preview info without saving.
Add a new Rootly integration after testing.

get.description:
List all Rootly integrations for the current user with permissions.

Permission caching:
- Returns cached permissions if < 8 hours old (instant response)
- Refreshes permissions if cache is stale or missing (slower, 10s timeout per integration)
- Users can manually refresh via the refresh button on integrations page
Check API permissions for a specific integration.

put.description: Update a Rootly integration name or set as default.

post.description: Manually refresh permissions for an integration (ignores cache).

get.description: Preview Rootly data without running full analysis.
Debug endpoint to show raw Rootly incident data and processing steps.

Fetch all users from a specific Rootly/PagerDuty integration.
Used to show team members who can submit burnout surveys.
Supports both numeric IDs and beta integration string IDs.

post.description:
Sync all users from a Rootly/PagerDuty integration to UserCorrelation table.

This ensures ALL team members can submit burnout surveys via Slack,
not just those who appear in incident data.

Returns sync statistics showing how many users were created/updated.

get.description:
Get list of users currently on-call from a Rootly/PagerDuty integration.
Returns emails of users who are currently on-call.
Used to highlight on-call users when selecting survey recipients.

Get all synced users from UserCorrelation table for the current user.
These are team members who can submit burnout surveys via Slack.
Optionally filter by integration_id to show only users from a specific organization.
Optionally include on-call status for each user (default: true).

post.description: Clear on-call cache and fetch fresh data for an integration.

put.description:
Save selected survey recipients for an integration.
These users will receive automated burnout survey invitations via Slack.

If recipient_ids is empty, this will RESET to default behavior (send to all users).

get.description:
Get saved survey recipients for an integration.
Returns list of UserCorrelation IDs that should receive surveys.

patch.description:
Manually update GitHub username for a UserCorrelation.
Used when automatic correlation fails and user needs to manually map.

Ensures that each GitHub username is assigned to only one user at a time.
If the same GitHub username is already assigned to another user, it will be removed from them.

Manually update Jira account mapping for a UserCorrelation.
Enforces exclusive one-to-one mapping across all users in the organization.

If ANY other user already has this Jira account_id, it will be removed from them first.
Used for dropdown selection in Team Members panel.

Manually update Linear user mapping for a UserCorrelation.
Enforces exclusive one-to-one mapping across all users in the organization.

If ANY other user already has this Linear user_id, it will be removed from them first.
Used for dropdown selection in Team Members panel.

Manually update Slack user mapping for a UserCorrelation.
Enforces exclusive one-to-one mapping across all users in the organization.

Args:
    slack_user_id: Slack user ID to assign. Empty string or whitespace clears the mapping.

Behavior:
    - Empty/whitespace slack_user_id: Clears the Slack mapping (sets to None)
    - Valid slack_user_id: Assigns to this user and removes from any other user

If ANY other user already has this Slack user_id, it will be removed from them first.
Used for dropdown selection in Team Members panel.

post.description: Test a PagerDuty API token and get account information.

get.description: Get all PagerDuty integrations for the current user.

schema.title: Response Get Pagerduty Integrations Pagerduty Integrations Get

post.description: Add a new PagerDuty integration.

Warm the permissions cache for all integrations in the background.

Called after login to pre-populate the permissions cache so that
subsequent operations (page loads, run analysis) are instant.

get.description:
Validate all integration connections before starting analysis.

Makes lightweight API calls to verify tokens are not expired/stale.
Returns validation status for each enabled integration (GitHub, Linear, Jira).

Args:
    force_refresh: If True, bypass cache and make fresh API calls

Rate limited to prevent abuse of third-party APIs.

post.description: Run a new burnout analysis for a specific integration and time range.

get.description:
List all previous analyses for the current user.

Optimized to use a single database query with window function for count,
avoiding separate COUNT(*) query overhead.
Get a specific analysis result by UUID.
Get a specific analysis result.
Get a specific analysis result by UUID or integer ID.

post.description: Regenerate daily trends data for an existing analysis.

get.description: Verify data consistency for an analysis across all components.
Get daily incident trends from the most recent analysis period.
Get daily incident trends from a specific analysis.

Get daily GitHub commit data for a specific user during an analysis period.

This endpoint fetches real-time GitHub commit data aggregated by day.

parameters.name: analysis_id

schema.description: Analysis ID to get date range from
schema.title: Analysis Id

parameters.description: Analysis ID to get date range from

get.description:
Get aggregated daily GitHub commit data for all team members in an analysis.

This endpoint returns commit data suitable for displaying a timeline chart,
similar to the incidents health trends chart.

Get individual daily health scores for a specific team member.

Returns real health data based on:
- Daily incident involvement
- Response time patterns  
- GitHub activity levels
- Slack communication patterns
- After-hours and weekend work

Test GitHub integration permissions and connectivity.
Also returns collected data summary for team members.

get.description: Get GitHub integration status for current user, including token validation.

Handles errors when user cancels or denies authorization.
Check what scopes the current Slack token has.

post.description: Test Slack integration permissions and connectivity.

This keeps the data but marks the workspace as inactive.

Debug endpoint to check if a user exists in user_correlations table.
If email not provided, checks current user's organization for all correlations.

Get current user's Slack information including email.
Useful for debugging user correlation issues.

post.description:
Handle /oncall-health slash command from Slack.
Opens a modal with the 3-question burnout survey.

Handle Slack interactive component submissions (modals, buttons, etc).
This is called when user submits the burnout survey modal.
Submit burnout survey response from Slack.

get.description: Get team survey response status for an analysis (manager view).

Diagnostic endpoint to check Slack workspace registration status.
Returns detailed information about workspace mappings and integrations.

post.description:
Manually register a Slack workspace that wasn't properly registered during OAuth.
This fixes the "workspace not registered" error for /oncall-health command.

Save manually provided Jira API token with validation and encryption.

Request body:
    token: str - The Jira Personal Access Token
    site_url: str - The Jira site URL (e.g., https://company.atlassian.net)
    user_info: dict (optional) - User info from frontend validation

Returns:
    Success response with integration details or error

Validate a Jira API token before saving to database.

Request body:
    token: str - The Jira Personal Access Token
    site_url: str - The Jira site URL (e.g., https://company.atlassian.net)

Returns:
    valid: bool - Whether the token is valid
    error: str | None - Error message if invalid
    error_type: str | None - Error category (authentication, permissions, network, format)
    help_url: str | None - Link to documentation
    action: str | None - Suggested next step
    user_info: dict | None - User display name, email if valid

get.description:
List all Jira workspaces/sites that the user has access to.
Returns the list of accessible resources with information about which one is currently selected.

post.description:
Select a specific Jira workspace/site to use.
Requires the cloud_id of the workspace to switch to.

- Refresh token if needed
- Permissions smoke test
- Fetch up to ~1000 “active” issues and log per-responder workload:
    * ticket count
    * priority distribution
    * earliest deadline (duedate)

Auto-map Jira users to team emails by email matching.

Records all mapping attempts to IntegrationMapping table for analysis tracking.
Similar to GitHub auto-mapping - uses email as primary identifier.

Accepts optional request body with:
- team_emails: List of source platform emails (e.g., from Rootly/PagerDuty)
- analysis_id: (Optional) Analysis that triggered this mapping
- source_platform: (Optional) Source platform name (default: "rootly")

Returns:
    Mapping statistics including success rate and per-user results

Sync all users from Jira workspace to UserCorrelation table.
Uses email-based matching first, falls back to fuzzy name matching.

Returns:
    Statistics about matched, created, updated, and skipped users

get.description:
Get all active Jira users from the connected workspace.
Used for dropdown selection in team member mapping interface.

Returns:
    List of Jira users with account_id, display_name, and email

post.description: Start Linear OAuth flow with PKCE.

get.description: Get Linear integration status, including token validation.

post.description:
Validate a Linear API key before saving to database.

Request body:
    token: str - The Linear Personal API Key

Returns:
    valid: bool - Whether the token is valid
    error: str | None - Error message if invalid
    error_type: str | None - Error category (authentication, permissions, network, format)
    help_url: str | None - Link to documentation
    action: str | None - Suggested next step
    user_info: dict | None - User display name, email if valid

Save manually provided Linear API key with validation and encryption.

Request body:
    token: str - The Linear Personal API Key (lin_api_...)
    user_info: dict (optional) - User info from frontend validation

Returns:
    Success response with integration details or error

get.summary: Get all manual mappings for current user
get.description: Get all manual mappings for the current user.

post.description:
Create a new manual mapping.

For GitHub and Jira mappings, automatically removes that account from any other users
to ensure each account is mapped to only one user at a time. This ensures that:
1. The account is removed from UserMapping table for other users
2. The account is removed from UserCorrelation table for other users
3. The new mapping is synced to UserCorrelation for the current user

put.description:
Update an existing mapping.

For GitHub and Jira mappings, automatically removes that account from any other users
to ensure each account is mapped to only one user at a time. This ensures that:
1. The account is removed from UserMapping table for other users
2. The account is removed from UserCorrelation table for other users
3. The updated mapping is synced to UserCorrelation for the current user

post.description: Mark a mapping as verified.
Bulk create multiple mappings.

get.description: Get mapping statistics for the current user.
Get mapping suggestions based on existing patterns.

schema.title: Response Get Mapping Suggestions Integrations Manual Mappings Suggestions Get

get.description: Get source identifiers that don't have mappings to target platform.

schema.description: If true, only return what would be cleaned up

parameters.description: If true, only return what would be cleaned up

schema.title: Remove Test Emails

parameters.description: If true, also remove emails with + symbols (test emails)

post.description: Run the enhanced GitHub mapping process for all unmapped users.

Run the Jira mapping process for all unmapped users using email-based matching.

Strategy:
1. Try exact email match first (case-insensitive)
2. Fall back to fuzzy name matching (>70% threshold)
3. Creates UserMapping records for persistent storage
4. Returns detailed results for frontend display

Run the Linear mapping process for all unmapped users using email-based matching.

Strategy:
1. Try exact email match first (case-insensitive)
2. Fall back to fuzzy name matching (>70% threshold)
3. Creates UserMapping records for persistent storage
4. Returns detailed results for frontend display

get.description: Check for mappings with NULL source_platform.

post.description: Fix mappings with NULL source_platform by setting them to 'rootly'.

Add integration_name and platform fields to analyses table

This can be called via API instead of running migration script directly

get.description: Debug what's in the most recent analysis
Check if migration has been run

post.description:
Refresh all demo analyses with the latest mock data.

This endpoint updates existing demo analyses and creates new ones for users
who don't have a demo analysis. Use this after updating mock_analysis_data.json
with new fields or data.

Security Requirements:
- ADMIN_API_KEY env var: Must be at least 32 characters (required)
- ADMIN_IP_WHITELIST env var: Comma-separated IPs/CIDRs (required)
- X-Admin-API-Key header: Must match ADMIN_API_KEY
- Rate limited to 5 requests/minute

Returns 503 if ADMIN_API_KEY or ADMIN_IP_WHITELIST is not properly configured.
Returns 403 if IP not whitelisted or API key doesn't match.

schema.title: Response Refresh Demo Analyses Api Admin Refresh Demo Analyses Post
